parameters.name: custinputorders
parameters.label: Custom Input Orders
parameters.description: This allows the input order, if provided, to be ignored and overridden by a custom order chosen by the following two parameters.

parameters.name: inxord
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description: Changing the Transform order will change where things go much the same way as going a block and turning east gets you to a different place than turning east and then going a block. In matrix math term

parameters.name: inrord
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description: As with transform order (above), changing the order in which the rotations take place will alter the final position and orientation. A Rotation order of Rx Ry Rz would create the final rotation matrix

parameters.name: input0preop
parameters.label: Input 0 Pre Operation
parameters.description: Operation(s) to apply on the transforms on Input 0, before they are combined with other transforms.

parameters.name: input1preop
parameters.label: Input 1 Pre Operation
parameters.description: Operation(s) to apply on the transforms on Input 1, before they are combined with other transforms.

parameters.name: inputoperation
parameters.label: Input Operation
parameters.description: The operation that should be applied between transforms coming from Input 0 and Input 1. Refer to the main description of this node for an explanation of how multiple samples and/or transform sets are

parameters.name: inputweight
parameters.label: Input Weight
parameters.description: Specify the Blend weight for the above 'Input Operation'

parameters.name: xord
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description: See description from earlier Transform Order parameter.

parameters.name: rord
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description: See description from earlier Rotate Order parameter.

parameters.name: lookat
parameters.label: Look At
parameters.description: Allows you to generate your transform channels by specifying an object you would like to Look At, or point to. The generated transform channels will be such that when you transform an object using it,

parameters.name: forwarddir
parameters.label: Forward Direction
parameters.description: Sets which axis and direction is considered the forward direction.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.label: Operation
parameters.description: Controls how the input transform(s) are combined with the transform specified on this page. The below two descriptions use a multiply "vector on the right" convention (column vectors).

parameters.name: weight
parameters.label: Weight
parameters.description: Specify the Blend weight for the above 'Operation'

parameters.name: postop
parameters.label: Post Operation
parameters.description: Optionally applied one last operation to the final generated transform before it is output.

parameters.name: output
parameters.label: Output
parameters.description: Specify the format the transform will be output in.

parameters.name: determ
parameters.label: Determinant
parameters.description: When outputting a matrix, it's determinant can also be output by enabling this parameter.

parameters.name: unmatchedchans
parameters.label: Un-matched Channels
parameters.description: Controls how channels that don't match the naming convention for the various transform format are treated.

parameters.name: custoutputorders
parameters.label: Custom Output Orders
parameters.description: By default the output transforms will use the orders given on teh Transform page. Enabling this allows for custom orders to be used for the transform that is output. This doesn't change the transform

parameters.name: outxord
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description: See description from earlier Transform Order parameter.

parameters.name: outrord
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description: See description from earlier Rotate Order parameter.

parameters.name: includeorderchans
parameters.label: Include Order Channels
parameters.description: Specified if the 'xord' and 'rord' channels should be output from this node. 'xord' will be output for 'Transform (Euler)' and 'Transform (Quaternion)' modes. 'rord' will be output for the 'Transform

parameters.name: continuousrotations
parameters.label: Continuous Rotations
parameters.description: In the case the input has multiple samples, this will attempt to keep rotations of neighbouring samples continuous. Basically, it tries to avoid 360 degree jumps. 360-> 361 instead of 360 -> 1 (which

parameters.name: usehint
parameters.label: Use Rotation Hint
parameters.description: An initial rotation hint given in r[xyz] degrees to try to stay continuous against. Turning this on and using the parameter below allows you to specify approximate starting values for the rotation cha

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page. See .

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).

parameters.name: upvectorz
parameters.label: Upvectorz
parameters.description: When orienting an object, the Up Vector is used to determine where the positive Y axis points.

parameters.name: hintz
parameters.label: Hintz
parameters.description: Specify approximate starting values for the rotation channels produced.

parameters.name: tz
parameters.label: Tz
parameters.description: XYZ translation values.

parameters.name: pz
parameters.label: Pz
parameters.description: XYZ pivot to apply the above operations around.

parameters.name: xformpagepreop
parameters.label: Xformpagepreop
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: sz
parameters.label: Sz
parameters.description: XYZ scale to shrink or enlarge the transform.

parameters.name: rz
parameters.label: Rz
parameters.description: XYZ rotation, in degrees.

parameters.name: trspreop
parameters.label: Trspreop
parameters.description: Help Not Available.
